title: stylidiaceae
intro: The Stylidiaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as triggerplants or trigger worts, characterized by their unique mechanism of attracting pollinators through a trigger-like action. This family consists of approximately 290 species, primarily found in Australia, but also in New Zealand, New Caledonia, and parts of Asia and the Pacific. The members of this family are typically low-growing, herbaceous plants that thrive in shaded, moist environments. Stylidiaceae plants are often recognized by their distinctive flowers, which have a unique shape and color, and their ability to trigger a movement in response to pollinators.
